examples.title: PDA for L = {0ⁿ1ⁿ | n ≥ 0} - Empty Stack Acceptance
examples.description: Algorithm to recognize balanced zeros and ones using stack
examples.explanation: PDA pushes 0 onto stack for each 0 read. When first 1 is encountered, switches to checking mode. For each 1, pops one 0. If stack becomes empty (Z0 reached) exactly when input ends, string is accepted.

examples.title: PDA for Palindromes - Non-deterministic
examples.description: NPDA for recognizing palindromes of form ww^R
examples.explanation: Non-determinism allows guessing when we've reached the middle of the string. Pushes first half, then non-deterministically switches to compare remaining input with stack contents in reverse order.

examples.title: General PDA Simulation Algorithm
examples.description: Generic algorithm to simulate PDA computation
examples.explanation: Simulation explores all possible non-deterministic paths. Uses BFS or DFS to explore configuration space. Accepts if any path leads to acceptance condition. NPDA may have exponential paths in worst case.
